review.annotator: human + assistant review of the RH20T global camera
review.method: Object world positions estimated by visual review of the RH20T cfg3 global camera (640x360 RGB; depth archive not extracted). The pen (mover) is tracked by its CENTROID from lying on the table ~13 cm beside the mesh pen holder (NEAR) to standing inside it (INSIDE); the holder (container) is static across the episode. Metric values estimated using the holder's ~8.5 cm diameter as the scale reference, in an arbitrary table-plane frame (origin offset is immaterial; only the pen-vs-holder relative geometry, read from the video, matters). Positions are NOT derived from the target CSG.
review.modelingNotes: The pen is modeled as a compact rigid object at its tracked centroid; its elongated shape and protrusion above the holder rim are NOT box-modeled, so the verdict is at the centroid-relation level (the pen's centroid moves from beside the holder into the holder volume, with the centroid below the rim). A strict full-bounding-box model of a 14 cm pen partially inserted in a ~9 cm holder would be a different, harder question; this smoke test asserts the centroid relation only.
review.provenance: Derived from a private same-content copy of RH20T_cfg3.tar.gz (own-OAuth-client Drive copy bypass); raw video stays off-repo. Source archive sha256 b49b297043f3ccf8386b620e11e9ccebc634ba5704e372ae7243480f6e38b6d3.
review.uncertaintyNotes: Oblique single camera, no depth; metric positions are review estimates (approx +/- 2-3 cm). The qualitative relations (initial NEAR / not-inside, terminal INSIDE) are clearly and unambiguously observed in the video; the exact centimetre values are approximate.

source.taskDescription: Put the pen into the pen holder
